judgment.summary: Tesla exhibits strong growth metrics and a healthy cash position but trades at a very high valuation with modest margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 17.2%; next earnings date is 2026-07-22.
positives: Revenue growth 15.8% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 16.6% indicate solid top-line momentum.
Analyst growth estimates of 23.4% for the current fiscal year suggest continued expansion.
A substantial $44.74B in cash provides significant liquidity and flexibility.
Free cash flow of $5.25B TTM demonstrates strong operational cash generation.
Consensus rating is 'buy' with a mean target price near the current stock level.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 385.26 and forward P/E of 165.79 indicate an extremely high valuation.
Operating margin of 4.2% and profit margin of 3.9% are relatively thin for the sector.
Return on equity of 4.9% and return on assets of 2.2% are modest.
High beta of 1.79 suggests significant volatility relative to the broader market.

news.paragraph: With no recent headlines, traders should focus on Tesla's upcoming earnings report on July 22nd. The stock's price action will likely be driven by management's commentary on production, demand, and competitive pressures in the EV market. Any forward-looking guidance will be critical for setting expectations into the second half of the year.
